# Generated by /batch-tasks on 2026-02-23
# Source: TODO.md — Orchestrator UI/UX Redesign (all P0+P1 tasks)
# 6 tasks with pre-generated plans
# 2 skipped: SQLite migration (score:28), P2/P3 (score:23) — see TODO.md P1+ sections
#
# Parallelism plan:
#   Group 1 (index.html): Task A
#   Group 2 (server.py):  Tasks B → C → D → E → F (serial, in order)
#   Groups 1+2 run concurrently.

===TASK===
model: sonnet
timeout: 1800
retries: 1
---
Redesign orchestrator/web/index.html to support Plan mode and Execute mode with a one-click mode toggle.

## Original TODO items
- Plan mode layout: terminal takes 70% width, right sidebar shows live Task Backlog
  - Task Backlog syncs automatically from proposed-tasks.md (via WebSocket already wired)
  - Tasks editable inline, draggable to reorder, × to delete
  - No separate "Import Proposed" button — file changes auto-populate the sidebar
- "⚡ Orchestrate" one-click button in Plan mode header
  - Sends /orchestrate\r to the active PTY
  - Goal input field next to it: user types goal → click → sends {goal}\r/orchestrate\r
- "Auto-start" toggle (default: ON)
  - When ON: proposed tasks auto-imported + all workers auto-started immediately
  - No overlay, no extra click in auto-start mode
  - When OFF: overlay appears for manual review (existing behavior)
- Execute mode layout: full-width worker dashboard, terminal hidden
  - Top bar: per-project progress bars (all open sessions)
  - Worker rows: task name | status | elapsed | commit hash | pushed? | [Log] [Retry]
  - [Intervene] button opens a slide-in terminal panel
- Mode toggle in header: [📋 Plan] [▶ Execute]

## Files to modify
- `orchestrator/web/index.html` — the only file; all changes here

## Key existing patterns (from reading the file)
- CSS variables at lines 11-26: --bg, --bg2, --bg3, --border, --text, --accent, etc.
- Current layout: `.chat-panel` (60%) + `.dashboard` (40%) at lines 89-94 and 232-237
- Header at lines 584-596: h1, #projectBadge button, .header-status div
- Chat panel content at lines 608-635: .chat-header, #terminalContainer, proposed overlay, .chat-input-row
- Dashboard at lines 638-686: queue section (641-660), workers section (662-668), progress footer, usage bar
- Proposed overlay logic at lines 1404-1445: showProposedOverlay(), confirmProposed()
- WebSocket status handler at lines 1047-1060: receives {type:'proposed_tasks'} → calls showProposedOverlay()
- `activeSessionId`, `sessionStates`, `PaneManager` manage terminal sessions
- `sendChat()` at line 1023: sends text+\r via active pane WebSocket — use same pattern for Orchestrate btn

## Steps

### CSS additions (add to <style> block, before closing </style>)
1. Add `.mode-btn` styles: tab-like buttons for [Plan] [Execute] toggle in header
   ```css
   .mode-btn {
     background: var(--bg3); border: 1px solid var(--border); border-radius: 5px;
     color: var(--text2); font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; padding: 4px 12px;
     cursor: pointer; transition: all 0.15s;
   }
   .mode-btn.active { background: var(--accent); color: #0d1117; border-color: var(--accent); }
   .mode-btn:hover:not(.active) { border-color: var(--accent); color: var(--text); }
   ```
2. Add `.goal-input` style for the goal text input next to Orchestrate button:
   ```css
   .goal-input {
     flex: 1; background: var(--bg3); border: 1px solid var(--border);
     border-radius: 5px; color: var(--text); font-size: 12px;
     font-family: var(--font-mono); padding: 4px 10px; outline: none;
     min-width: 0; transition: border-color 0.15s;
   }
   .goal-input:focus { border-color: var(--accent); }
   .goal-input::placeholder { color: var(--text2); }
   ```
3. In execute mode the dashboard takes full width. Add:
   ```css
   .main.execute-mode .chat-panel { display: none; }
   .main.execute-mode .dashboard { width: 100%; }
   .main.plan-mode .chat-panel { width: 70%; }
   .main.plan-mode .dashboard { width: 30%; }
   ```
4. Add slide-in terminal panel for Execute mode intervention:
   ```css
   .intervene-panel {
     position: fixed; right: 0; top: 0; bottom: 0; width: 50vw;
     background: var(--bg2); border-left: 1px solid var(--border);
     display: flex; flex-direction: column; z-index: 150;
     transform: translateX(100%); transition: transform 0.25s ease;
   }
   .intervene-panel.open { transform: translateX(0); }
   .intervene-header {
     padding: 10px 14px; border-bottom: 1px solid var(--border);
     display: flex; align-items: center; justify-content: space-between;
     background: var(--bg2); font-size: 13px; font-weight: 600;
   }
   .intervene-terminal { flex: 1; overflow: hidden; }
   ```

### HTML changes
5. In `<header>` (line 584), after the `<h1>` tag and before `#projectBadge`, add the mode toggle buttons:
   ```html
   <div style="display:flex;gap:4px;margin-left:8px">
     <button class="mode-btn active" id="modePlanBtn" onclick="setMode('plan')" title="Plan mode: orchestrate tasks">📋 Plan</button>
     <button class="mode-btn" id="modeExecBtn" onclick="setMode('execute')" title="Execute mode: monitor workers">▶ Execute</button>
   </div>
   ```
6. In `.chat-header` (lines 609-613), replace the span content with:
   ```html
   <span class="dot yellow" id="chatDot"></span>
   <input class="goal-input" id="goalInput" placeholder="Describe your goal…" autocomplete="off" />
   <button class="btn" id="orchestrateBtn" onclick="sendOrchestrate()" style="white-space:nowrap;font-size:12px;padding:4px 12px">⚡ Orchestrate</button>
   <label style="display:flex;align-items:center;gap:5px;font-size:11px;color:var(--text2);cursor:pointer;margin-left:8px;white-space:nowrap" title="Auto-start workers when tasks are proposed">
     <input type="checkbox" id="autoStartToggle" checked style="cursor:pointer" />
     Auto-start
   </label>
   <span style="margin-left:auto;font-size:11px;opacity:0.6">PTY</span>
   ```
7. Add the Intervene panel HTML after the closing `</div>` of `.main` (line 687), before the Worker Log Modal:
   ```html
   <!-- Intervene Panel (Execute mode slide-in terminal) -->
   <div class="intervene-panel" id="intervenePanel">
     <div class="intervene-header">
       <span>Intervene — Orchestrator Terminal</span>
       <button class="btn small secondary" onclick="closeIntervene()">✕ Close</button>
     </div>
     <div class="intervene-terminal" id="interveneTerminal"></div>
   </div>
   ```
8. In Execute mode the workers section header needs a [Merge All Done] button — update the workers section-header div (around line 664) to add:
   ```html
   <button class="btn small success" id="mergeAllBtn" onclick="mergeAllDone()" style="display:none">⬇ Merge All Done</button>
   ```

### JavaScript additions (add before closing </script>)
9. Mode switching:
   ```javascript
   // ─── Mode switching ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   let currentMode = 'plan';
   function setMode(mode) {
     currentMode = mode;
     const main = document.querySelector('.main');
     main.classList.toggle('plan-mode', mode === 'plan');
     main.classList.toggle('execute-mode', mode === 'execute');
     document.getElementById('modePlanBtn').classList.toggle('active', mode === 'plan');
     document.getElementById('modeExecBtn').classList.toggle('active', mode === 'execute');
     if (mode === 'plan') {
       closeIntervene();
       requestAnimationFrame(() => PaneManager.fitAll());
     }
     localStorage.setItem('orchestrator_mode', mode);
   }
   // Restore mode on load
   const _savedMode = localStorage.getItem('orchestrator_mode');
   if (_savedMode) setMode(_savedMode);
   else document.querySelector('.main').classList.add('plan-mode');
   ```
10. Orchestrate button handler:
    ```javascript
    function sendOrchestrate() {
      const goal = document.getElementById('goalInput').value.trim();
      const st = sessionStates.get(activeSessionId);
      if (!st || !st.activePaneId) return;
      const pane = st.panes.get(st.activePaneId);
      if (!pane || !pane.ws || pane.ws.readyState !== WebSocket.OPEN) return;
      if (goal) {
        pane.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'input', data: goal + '\r' }));
        setTimeout(() => {
          pane.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'input', data: '/orchestrate\r' }));
        }, 100);
      } else {
        pane.ws.send(JSON.stringify({ type: 'input', data: '/orchestrate\r' }));
      }
      document.getElementById('goalInput').value = '';
    }
    document.getElementById('goalInput')?.addEventListener('keydown', e => {
      if (e.key === 'Enter' && !e.shiftKey) { e.preventDefault(); sendOrchestrate(); }
    });
    ```
11. Modify the `ws.onmessage` handler in `connectStatus()` (around line 1047). In the `proposed_tasks` branch, check auto-start toggle:
    ```javascript
    else if (msg.type === 'proposed_tasks') {
      if (msg.content && msg.content.includes('===TASK===')) {
        window._lastProposedContent = msg.content;
        const autoStart = document.getElementById('autoStartToggle')?.checked;
        if (autoStart) {
          // Auto-mode: import + start immediately, no overlay
          fetch(`/api/tasks/import-proposed?session=${activeSessionId}`, { method: 'POST',
            headers: {'Content-Type':'application/json'},
            body: JSON.stringify({ content: msg.content })
          }).then(() => fetch(`/api/tasks/start-all?session=${activeSessionId}`, { method: 'POST' }))
            .then(r => r.json())
            .then(d => {
              setMode('execute');
              showToast(`Auto-started ${d.started} worker${d.started !== 1 ? 's' : ''}`);
            });
        } else {
          showProposedOverlay(msg.content);
        }
      }
    }
    ```
12. Intervene panel (uses active session's existing terminal pane):
    ```javascript
    function openIntervene() {
      document.getElementById('intervenePanel').classList.add('open');
      // Reuse the existing session terminal — move it into intervene-terminal
      const st = sessionStates.get(activeSessionId);
      if (st) {
        const interveneTerm = document.getElementById('interveneTerminal');
        interveneTerm.appendChild(st.el);
        st.el.style.display = 'flex';
        requestAnimationFrame(() => PaneManager.fitSession(activeSessionId));
      }
    }
    function closeIntervene() {
      document.getElementById('intervenePanel').classList.remove('open');
      // Move terminal back to terminalContainer
      const st = sessionStates.get(activeSessionId);
      const container = document.getElementById('terminalContainer');
      if (st && container) {
        container.appendChild(st.el);
        if (currentMode === 'plan') st.el.style.display = 'flex';
      }
    }
    ```
13. Merge All Done button (stub — wires to future backend API):
    ```javascript
    async function mergeAllDone() {
      showToast('Merge feature coming soon — push from your terminal for now');
    }
    ```
14. Show Merge All Done button in Execute mode when there are done workers — modify `renderWorkers()`:
    After `document.getElementById('workerCount').textContent = ...`, add:
    ```javascript
    const doneCount = workers.filter(w => w.status === 'done' && w.auto_committed).length;
    const mergeBtn = document.getElementById('mergeAllBtn');
    if (mergeBtn) mergeBtn.style.display = doneCount > 0 && currentMode === 'execute' ? '' : 'none';
    ```
15. Add [Intervene] button to Execute mode header — in the section-header for workers, add:
    ```html
    <button class="btn small secondary" onclick="openIntervene()" id="interveneBtn" style="display:none">⌨ Intervene</button>
    ```
    Show it in execute mode: in `setMode()`, add:
    ```javascript
    const intervBtn = document.getElementById('interveneBtn');
    if (intervBtn) intervBtn.style.display = mode === 'execute' ? '' : 'none';
    ```

## Self-review (do this before finishing)
1. Re-read the original requirements above — did you implement everything?
2. Re-read every file you changed — look for logic bugs, edge cases (null, empty arrays, missing imports), typos
3. Check that setMode('plan') and setMode('execute') both work without JS errors
4. Verify auto-start toggle: when ON, proposed tasks should start without overlay; when OFF, overlay shows
5. Verify Orchestrate button: with goal text sends goal then /orchestrate; without goal just sends /orchestrate
6. Verify that switching tabs (sessions) still works correctly after mode CSS changes
7. Open the page in browser devtools (or check HTML structure) — confirm .main gets plan-mode/execute-mode class


===TASK===
model: haiku
timeout: 1800
retries: 2
---
Add auto-push to feature branch after each successful commit in orchestrator/server.py.

## Original TODO items
- After verify_and_commit() succeeds: immediately push to origin orchestrator/task-{id}
- Show "✓ committed · ✓ pushed" vs "✓ committed · ⏳ pushing" in worker status
- Code is safe on remote the moment it's done

## Files to modify
- `orchestrator/server.py` only

## Key existing code (already read)
- Worker class has: `self.auto_committed: bool = False`, `self.verified: bool = False`
- `verify_and_commit()` is at lines 348-436. After line 425 (`self.auto_committed = True`), add push logic.
- Worker.__init__ at line 205: add `self.auto_pushed: bool = False` and `self.branch_name: str | None = None`
- `Worker.to_dict()` at line 238-261: add `auto_pushed` to the returned dict

## Steps
1. In `Worker.__init__()` (around line 229), add two new instance variables after `self.auto_committed`:
   ```python
   self.auto_pushed: bool = False
   self.branch_name: str | None = None
   ```
2. In `Worker.to_dict()` (lines 248-261), add `"auto_pushed": self.auto_pushed` and `"branch_name": self.branch_name` to the returned dict.
3. In `Worker.verify_and_commit()`, after the block starting at line 424 (`if commit_proc.returncode == 0:`), after updating `self.last_commit`, add push logic:
   ```python
   # Auto-push to feature branch
   branch = f"orchestrator/task-{self.task_id}"
   self.branch_name = branch
   push_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_shell(
       f'git push origin HEAD:{branch} --force-with-lease',
       stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE, stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
       cwd=str(self._project_dir),
   )
   try:
       p_out, p_err = await asyncio.wait_for(push_proc.communicate(), timeout=30)
       if push_proc.returncode == 0:
           self.auto_pushed = True
   except asyncio.TimeoutError:
       pass
   ```
4. In the UI commit display in the frontend (index.html line ~1215-1218), the `auto_committed` flag already drives display. The `auto_pushed` field is now available in the status broadcast — the frontend can use it. For now just expose it in to_dict(); the frontend task (Task A) already handles display.

## Self-review (do this before finishing)
1. Verify the push command uses `HEAD:{branch}` format (pushes current HEAD to named remote branch)
2. Confirm `--force-with-lease` is used (safe push — fails if remote has diverged unexpectedly)
3. Confirm `auto_pushed` is in `to_dict()` output
4. Make sure push timeout (30s) won't block the main loop — it's in an async subprocess, so it won't


===TASK===
model: sonnet
timeout: 1800
retries: 2
---
Add failure context capture and retry-with-context to orchestrator/server.py Workers.

## Original TODO items
- Capture failure context: when worker exits non-zero, read last 50 lines of log as error_summary
- Store as task attribute: failed_reason
- Retry task description = original description + failure context, "Do NOT repeat same approach"
- Add POST /api/tasks/retry-failed endpoint: requeues all failed tasks with error context injected
- "Retry All Failed" button already planned in UI Task A; this task adds the backend API

## Files to modify
- `orchestrator/server.py` only

## Key existing code
- `Worker.poll()` at lines 327-346: sets `self.status = "done" if rc == 0 else "failed"` at line 333
- `Worker._log_path` is the path to the log file (set in Worker.start() line 266)
- `WorkerPool.poll_all()` at lines 469-498: calls `w.poll()` and then checks `w.status in ("done","failed")`
- `TaskQueue.add()` at lines 95-111: creates a new task
- `app.post("/api/tasks/retry-failed")` doesn't exist yet — add it after the existing task endpoints

## Steps
1. Add `failure_context: str | None = None` to Worker.__init__() (after `self.auto_pushed` or near other bool attrs around line 229).

2. Add `"failure_context": self.failure_context` to `Worker.to_dict()`.

3. In `Worker.poll()` (lines 327-346), after setting `self.status = "failed"` (line 333 area), add:
   ```python
   if self.status == "failed" and self._log_path and self._log_path.exists():
       try:
           text = self._log_path.read_text(errors="replace")
           lines = [l for l in text.splitlines() if l.strip()]
           self.failure_context = "\n".join(lines[-50:])
       except Exception:
           pass
   ```

4. In `WorkerPool.poll_all()` (lines 469-498), update the task_queue.update call for failed workers to include failure_context. After the `await task_queue.update(w.task_id, status=w.status, ...)` call for done/failed workers (around line 487), add:
   ```python
   if w.status == "failed" and w.failure_context:
       await task_queue.update(w.task_id, failed_reason=w.failure_context)
   ```

5. Add a new REST endpoint after line 935 (after `start_all` endpoint):
   ```python
   @app.post("/api/tasks/retry-failed")
   async def retry_failed(s: ProjectSession = Depends(_resolve_session)):
       """Requeue all failed tasks with their failure context injected."""
       tasks = await s.task_queue.list()
       failed = [t for t in tasks if t["status"] == "failed"]
       retried = []
       for t in failed:
           failed_reason = t.get("failed_reason", "")
           retry_desc = t["description"]
           if failed_reason:
               retry_desc += (
                   f"\n\n---\n**Previous attempt failed. Error context:**\n```\n{failed_reason[:2000]}\n```\n"
                   "Do NOT repeat the same approach. Analyze the error above and try a different strategy."
               )
           new_task = await s.task_queue.add(retry_desc, t.get("model", "sonnet"))
           retried.append(new_task["id"])
       return {"retried": len(retried), "task_ids": retried}
   ```

6. Also update `TaskQueue.update()` to accept `failed_reason` kwarg — it already uses `**kwargs` so `t.update(kwargs)` will work. No change needed there as long as `failed_reason` is added to the task dict on `add()`. Actually, since `add()` doesn't initialize `failed_reason`, it won't appear in existing tasks. Add `"failed_reason": None` to the task dict in `TaskQueue.add()` (around line 108, in the `task = { ... }` dict).

## Self-review (do this before finishing)
1. Verify failure_context is populated ONLY for failed workers (not done)
2. Verify the retry endpoint creates new tasks (not modifying the failed task in place)
3. Verify the retry task description safely truncates failure context at 2000 chars to avoid oversized prompts
4. Test edge case: worker that failed with empty log (failure_context stays None, retry still works)


===TASK===
model: haiku
timeout: 1800
retries: 2
---
Auto-inject each project's CLAUDE.md content into worker task descriptions in orchestrator/server.py.

## Original TODO items
- When a task is created for a project, prepend the project's .claude/CLAUDE.md content
- Worker knows the stack, conventions, and what NOT to do before it starts

## Files to modify
- `orchestrator/server.py` only

## Key existing code
- `Worker.__init__()` at line 205: takes `project_dir` and `claude_dir` as Path args
- `Worker.start()` at line 263: writes `self.description` to task file at line 271 (`task_file.write_text(self.description)`)
- `self._claude_dir` is `project_dir / ".claude"` (set in __init__ line 218)

## Steps
1. In `Worker.start()` (lines 263+), before line 271 (`task_file.write_text(self.description)`), add CLAUDE.md injection:
   ```python
   # Prepend project CLAUDE.md for context
   effective_description = self.description
   claude_md = self._claude_dir / "CLAUDE.md"
   if claude_md.exists():
       try:
           claude_content = claude_md.read_text(errors="replace").strip()
           if claude_content:
               effective_description = (
                   f"# Project Context (from .claude/CLAUDE.md)\n\n{claude_content}\n\n"
                   f"---\n\n# Task\n\n{self.description}"
               )
       except Exception:
           pass
   task_file.write_text(effective_description)
   ```

   Remove the existing `task_file.write_text(self.description)` at line 271 since it's now replaced above.

## Self-review (do this before finishing)
1. Confirm that `effective_description` (not `self.description`) is written to the task file
2. Confirm `self.description` is NOT modified in place — only the written file content changes (self.description is still used for display, commit messages, etc.)
3. Confirm it gracefully handles missing CLAUDE.md (file doesn't exist → normal behavior)
4. Confirm it gracefully handles empty CLAUDE.md (if stripped content is empty, skip injection)


===TASK===
model: sonnet
timeout: 1800
retries: 1
---
Implement git worktree isolation per worker in orchestrator/server.py.

## Original TODO items
- Each worker spawns in its own git worktree: git worktree add .claude/worktrees/worker-{id} -b orchestrator/task-{id}
- Worker's cwd is the worktree path (not main repo)
- On worker done/failed: cleanup the worktree (git worktree remove --force)
- Workers in different sessions share the same repo — worktrees provide true parallel isolation

## Files to modify
- `orchestrator/server.py` only

## Key existing code
- Worker.__init__() at line 205: sets `self._project_dir`
- Worker.start() at line 263: uses `cwd=str(self._project_dir)` for subprocess (line 284)
- Worker.stop() at lines 314-325: terminates process
- Worker.poll() at line 327: uses `cwd=str(self._project_dir)` for git log
- Worker.verify_and_commit() at line 348: uses `cwd=str(self._project_dir)` extensively

## Important design constraint
- The worktree directory needs to be OUTSIDE the main repo to avoid nested git issues
- Use: `self._claude_dir / "worktrees" / f"worker-{self.id}"` which is `.claude/worktrees/worker-X` — this is INSIDE the repo but .gitignore should handle it. Better: use sibling dir.
- Actually: git worktrees CAN be inside the repo directory as long as they're gitignored. `.claude/` is typically gitignored. Use `.claude/worktrees/worker-{id}`.

## Steps
1. In Worker.__init__() (line 205), add:
   ```python
   self._worktree_path: Path | None = None
   self._branch_name: str | None = None
   ```

2. In Worker.to_dict() (line 238), add `"worktree_path": str(self._worktree_path) if self._worktree_path else None` to the returned dict.

3. In Worker.start() (line 263), BEFORE the existing log setup, add worktree creation:
   ```python
   # Create isolated git worktree for this worker
   worktree_base = self._claude_dir / "worktrees"
   worktree_base.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   self._worktree_path = worktree_base / f"worker-{self.id}"
   self._branch_name = f"orchestrator/task-{self.task_id}"

   wt_proc = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
       "git", "worktree", "add", str(self._worktree_path), "-b", self._branch_name,
       stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE, stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
       cwd=str(self._project_dir),
   )
   try:
       wt_out, wt_err = await asyncio.wait_for(wt_proc.communicate(), timeout=30)
       if wt_proc.returncode == 0:
           # Worker runs from the worktree — true isolation
           self._project_dir = self._worktree_path
       else:
           # Worktree creation failed (e.g. branch exists) — fall back to main dir
           err = wt_err.decode().strip()
           # Try without -b if branch already exists
           wt_proc2 = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
               "git", "worktree", "add", str(self._worktree_path), f"HEAD",
               stdout=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE, stderr=asyncio.subprocess.PIPE,
               cwd=str(self._project_dir),
           )
           wt_out2, _ = await asyncio.wait_for(wt_proc2.communicate(), timeout=30)
           if wt_proc2.returncode == 0:
               self._project_dir = self._worktree_path
   except Exception:
       pass  # On any failure, worker runs from main repo (degraded but functional)
   ```

4. In Worker.stop() (lines 314-325), after the kill logic and before `self.status = "done"`, add worktree cleanup:
   ```python
   # Clean up worktree
   if self._worktree_path and self._worktree_path.exists():
       cleanup = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
           "git", "worktree", "remove", "--force", str(self._worktree_path),
           stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
           cwd=str(self._claude_dir.parent),  # run from original project dir
       )
       try:
           await asyncio.wait_for(cleanup.communicate(), timeout=15)
       except Exception:
           pass
   ```

5. Also add worktree cleanup in Worker.poll() when it detects the process has finished (status becomes done/failed). Add after line 333 (status assignment):
   ```python
   if self.status in ("done", "failed") and self._worktree_path and self._worktree_path.exists():
       asyncio.ensure_future(self._cleanup_worktree())
   ```
   Add new method to Worker class:
   ```python
   async def _cleanup_worktree(self) -> None:
       if not self._worktree_path:
           return
       cleanup = await asyncio.create_subprocess_exec(
           "git", "worktree", "remove", "--force", str(self._worktree_path),
           stdout=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL, stderr=asyncio.subprocess.DEVNULL,
           cwd=str(self._claude_dir.parent),
       )
       try:
           await asyncio.wait_for(cleanup.communicate(), timeout=15)
       except Exception:
           pass
       self._worktree_path = None
   ```

6. Make sure `.claude/worktrees/` is gitignored. Add to .gitignore in the project root if not already present:
   ```
   .claude/worktrees/
   ```

## Self-review (do this before finishing)
1. Verify that when worktree creation SUCCEEDS, `self._project_dir` is updated to the worktree path
2. Verify that when worktree creation FAILS, the worker still runs (graceful degradation)
3. Verify cleanup runs in both Worker.stop() and Worker.poll() (process dying naturally)
4. Make sure `self._claude_dir.parent` is used as cwd for worktree commands (original project dir)
5. Check that `git worktree add` doesn't fail if worktrees/ dir doesn't exist — the mkdir handles this


===TASK===
model: haiku
timeout: 1800
retries: 2
---
Add "Retry All Failed" button to orchestrator/web/index.html Execute mode.

## Original TODO items
- One click to requeue all failed tasks with their error context injected
- Backend API POST /api/tasks/retry-failed is added by the server.py task (Task C)
- This task adds the UI button

## Files to modify
- `orchestrator/web/index.html` only

## Key existing code
- Workers section-header at lines 663-666: `<div class="section-header"><span>Workers ...</span></div>`
- `runAllTasks()` at line 1447: pattern to follow for the new function
- `activeSessionId` global variable used in all fetch calls
- Worker status values: "running", "paused", "blocked", "done", "failed", "starting"
- `workers` array at line 749 contains current worker states

## Steps
1. In the workers section-header div (around line 664), add the Retry All Failed button after the workerCount span:
   ```html
   <button class="btn small danger" id="retryFailedBtn" onclick="retryAllFailed()" style="display:none">↺ Retry Failed</button>
   ```

2. Add the JS function (add near `runAllTasks()` function, around line 1447):
   ```javascript
   async function retryAllFailed() {
     const res = await fetch(`/api/tasks/retry-failed?session=${activeSessionId}`, { method: 'POST' });
     const data = await res.json();
     showToast(`Requeued ${data.retried} failed task${data.retried !== 1 ? 's' : ''} with error context`);
   }
   ```

3. In `renderWorkers()` (lines 1205-1254), after the `workerCount` update, add logic to show/hide the retry button:
   ```javascript
   const failedCount = workers.filter(w => w.status === 'failed').length;
   const retryBtn = document.getElementById('retryFailedBtn');
   if (retryBtn) retryBtn.style.display = failedCount > 0 ? '' : 'none';
   ```

## Self-review (do this before finishing)
1. Verify the button shows only when there are failed workers, hides otherwise
2. Verify the fetch URL matches the backend endpoint: POST /api/tasks/retry-failed
3. Verify toast message shows the retried count correctly
4. Verify button ID and onclick match exactly
